concerns.text: The plan cites 's3://modis-landsat-las' as the AWS Open Data Registry for MODIS (MOD11A1). This bucket path is incorrect/non-existent for the standard MODIS LST product. The canonical AWS Open Data for MODIS is located at 's3://nasa-modis' (or via the specific 'MODIS/061/MOD11A1' prefix). Using an invalid bucket path will cause the ingestion pipeline to fail immediately, preventing the acquisition of the target variable (LST). The plan must cite the correct canonical S3 path or the specific Earthdata Search URL for MOD11A1.

The plan cites 's3://landsat-pds' for Landsat 8 LST. While this bucket exists, it hosts Level-1 (radiance) or Level-2 (Surface Reflectance/Temp) products in specific prefixes (e.g., 's3://landsat-pds/c1/L8/'). The plan does not specify the exact prefix for Level-2 Surface Temperature (ST_B10) required for direct LST analysis without complex atmospheric correction. Relying on the root bucket without a specific path for the thermal band (Band 10) risks ingesting the wrong data type (e.g., raw radiance) or failing to locate the pre-processed LST product, violating the 'Verified Accuracy' constitution principle.
